contents.description: George finds the ideal napping spot at work; Jerry has his kitchen rebuilt; Elaine meets a new beau (Vince Grant).
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: George begins to wonder what his new girlfriend (Suzanne Cryer) isn't telling him when she says, "Yada, yada, yada."
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: Newman nixes Kramer's millennium-party plans; Jerry obsesses about his position on his girlfriend's speed dial.
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: Elaine's new business, selling only the upper part of muffins, is challenged to dispose of the unused bottoms.
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: Viewers are taken for a behind-the-scenes look at a "Family Guy" production week; Peter is replaced and pitches a new series to Fox Television Group Chairmen and CEO's Dana Walden and Gary Newman.
contents.title: Family Guy

contents.description: Taylor Swift agrees to go to the homecoming dance with Chris; Peter decides to become an Uber driver.
contents.episodeTitle: Chris Has Got a Date, Date, Date, Date, Date
contents.title: Family Guy

contents.description: Peter and Lois decide not to vaccinate Stewie after reading about the potential side effects; the rest of Quahog soon follows suit.
contents.title: Family Guy

contents.description: The cast re-enacts "The Great Gatsby," "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn" and "Of Mice and Men."
contents.title: Family Guy

contents.description: When Peter overhears Carter's plan to save money by using toxic chemicals in the brewery's beer cans, he tells a journalist about it; Brian asks for Stewie's help when his driver's license expired.
contents.title: Family Guy

contents.description: Peter gets power hungry when he's asked to fill in for a mall Santa; Stewie and Brian decide to crash holiday office parties for free food, fun and women.
contents.title: Family Guy

contents.description: South Park citizens contend with widespread use of a racial slur.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Hillary Clinton comes to South Park for a campaign rally; Cartman follows a lead on a possible terrorist attack.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Stan searches for the real story behind eggs and bunnies in the Easter ritual.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: When Ms. Garrison is the victim of another failed relationship, she takes it out on the fourth-graders.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: The boys attempt to find a solution to the increasing number of homeless people in South Park.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Cartman discovers the joys of having Tourette's syndrome; drunk with the power of saying whatever he wants without getting in trouble for it, he lines up National TV Coverage to take advantage of his new life with no filters.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Randy becomes a local hero when he thinks that he may have broken a world record.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Stan's superior skills on "Guitar Hero" damage his friendship with Kyle.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: A secret list created by the girls in the fourth grade, rates every boy's looks from cutest to ugliest.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Cartman comes face to face with his own mortality when his routine tonsillectomy goes horribly wrong.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Cleveland volunteers to take Roberta on a college visit to his alma mater as an excuse to relive his college days; Rallo helps Cleveland Jr. learn to sleep without a stuffed animal.
contents.title: The Cleveland Show

contents.description: Cleveland Jr. goes on a church trip to impress the girl he likes; Donna's ex-boyfriend returns from Iraq to find her married.
contents.title: The Cleveland Show

contents.description: Cleveland Jr. loses respect for his father when Cleveland falls off the toilet and gets a concussion; Rallo is finally tall enough to ride his favorite roller coaster.
contents.title: The Cleveland Show

contents.description: When an unexpected event leads Donna's estranged mother to find Cleveland, he arranges to reunite the two women.
contents.title: The Cleveland Show

contents.description: When the boys help Britney Spears get to the North Pole, they discover the shocking secret behind her popularity.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: In a South Park homage to the 1981 film, Heavy Metal, the boys are trying to get Kenny off the latest drug craze that's captured the junior high and under set.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: The head of the World Canadian Bureau leads the country into a strike and the responsibility of brokering a settlement rests with the boys.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: While Ms. Garrison is off trying to find a way to become the man he was always intended to be, Cartman is put in charge of the classroom.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Randy packs up his family and heads west when he wakes up to find the Internet is gone.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: While the kids are on a field trip to a living museum, Cartman makes Butters sneak away from the class to go to the amusement center next door.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Butters stands with Cartman as he confronts the Chinese after a tragic event.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: Wendy gets in trouble when she threatens to beat up Cartman after school.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: While the country celebrates the outcome of the election, the new President-elect arrives at the White House prematurely.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: The boys must embrace the latest fad to hit South Park Elementary or risk their status as the coolest kids in school.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: The Goth Kids are angry when the other children can't tell the difference between a goth and a vampire.
contents.title: South Park

contents.description: George's plans for a summer getaway are foiled; Elaine's well-meaning criticism of a co-worker is not well-received.
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: Competing comic Bania warms to Jerry's crowds; Elaine and David unite for a European trip; Kramer reconsiders butter.
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: Jerry finds part of his new date's anatomy of interest; George's job is threatened; Elaine's relationship sputters.
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: Jerry tries to reveal more of his feelings on the advice of his new girlfriend (Lori Loughlin). With Jason Alexander.
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: At his parents' insistence, Jerry is coached by personal trainer Izzy Mandelbaum (Lloyd Bridges) to lose weight.
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: Jerry, promised a vehicle as payment for a performance, winds up getting a van instead of a car; Elaine meets a new man; Kramer has no need for mail.
contents.title: Seinfeld

contents.description: Michael takes the Dunder Mifflin employees ice skating to celebrate his birthday; Kevin waits nervously for test results.
contents.title: The Office

contents.description: Dwight finds half a joint in the parking lot and launches a full investigation; Michael asks an unusual favor.
contents.title: The Office

contents.description: Chaos ensues when Michael takes responsibility for conflict resolution from the Human Relations department.
contents.title: The Office

contents.description: Employees take some big gambles when they host a charity casino-night in their warehouse.
contents.title: The Office

contents.description: Michael unintentionally outs a gay employee; the situation with Jim and Pam.
contents.title: The Office

contents.description: Michael organizes a party in his hotel room when he, Dwight and Jan attend the Northeastern Mid-Market Office Supply Convention in Philadelphia.
contents.title: The Office
